q: In the trial behind its FDA approval for preventing COVID-19 after exposure, by what percentage did ensitrelvir reduce the risk of symptomatic infection versus placebo?
ans: The trial (SCORPIO-PEP) found that ensitrelvir reduced the risk of symptomatic COVID-19 in household contacts by **2.4% vs. 9.9% in the placebo group**. This results in a **7.5% relative risk reduction** (9.9% - 2.4% = 7.5%). The findings are reported in the *New England Journal of Medicine* (URL: [https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2509306](https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2509306)).

q: Which organ in the human body produces insulin?
ans:
The organ that produces insulin in the human body is the **pancreas**. Insulin is produced by the **islets of Langerhans** within the pancreas, which regulate blood sugar levels by promoting glucose uptake in cells. 

This information is based on standard biological knowledge and confirmed by reliable sources.

q: What is the only planet in our solar system that rotates on its side?
ans: The only planet in our solar system that rotates on its side is **Venus**. It rotates retrograde (opposite to most other planets), but this is not exactly "on its side" in the traditional sense. Instead, it spins in the opposite direction of its orbit. However, if the question refers to retrograde rotation, Venus is the only planet with this characteristic.
